reasoning: Sheikh Hasina fled to India in August 2024 and currently faces over 100 criminal cases in Bangladesh, including 42 for murder, along with active arrest warrants from the International Crimes Tribunal. Under these circumstances, returning voluntarily before July 1, 2025, would almost certainly lead to immediate arrest and imprisonment, making it highly unlikely she will do so unless the interim government somehow collapses. On the extradition front, while Bangladesh formally requested her return in December 2024, India has not acted on this request as of early May 2025. India views Hasina as a close ally and has both political and legal reasons to stall. Even if India were to process the extradition, Hasina would have the right to challenge it in Indian courts, a process that historically takes months or years. Furthermore, the time window for this question is extremely tight (before July 1, 2025). Given the slow pace of international extradition and her overwhelming disincentive to return voluntarily, the probability of her entering Bangladesh before July 2025 is at the floor.
